data.title: Catalyst Watch: Eyes on oil prices, inflation reads, and Oracle earnings
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - March 9

  • All week - The energy markets will be closely watched as developments in the Middle East conflict play out.
  • Volatility watch - IREN (IREN) and Xenon Pharmaceuticals (XENE) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include White Pearl Acquisition (WPAC), Peakstone Realty Trust (PKST), and Archrock (AROC). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Horizon Technology (HRZN), Fulgent Genetics (FLGT), and Franklin Covey (FC). Short interest is elevated on Hertz Global (HTZ) and Six Flags Entertainment (FUN).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Hewlett Packard Enterprises (HPE), Casey's General Stores (CASY), and Vail Resorts (MTN). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Voyager Technologies (VOYG) after it reports.
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Alphabet (GOOG), FedEx (FDX), Travelers (TRV), and Coca-Cola (KO).
  • IPO watch - PayPay (PAYP) is expected to price its IPO and begin trading. The IPO lockup expires on certain blocks of shares of Klarna (KLAR), Black Rock Coffee Bar (BRCB), Gemini Space Station (GEMI), and Via Transportation (VIA). The quiet period expires on Solv Energy (MWH) and Arko Petroleum (APC) to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • All day - The three-day Deutsche Bank Media, Internet, and Telecom Conference begins. Some of the notable companies with presentations or fireside chats include American Tower (AMT), Verizon (VZ), and Roku (ROKU).
  • All day - The Game Developers Conference in San Francisco is expected to see a broader AI-focused festival atmosphere. Nvidia (NVDA), Apple (AAPL), and Ubisoft (UBSFY) are some of the companies that will have a significant presence.
  • All day - Shareholders with Alexander & Baldwin (ALEX) will vote on the takeover offer from a joint venture of MW Group, Blackstone Real Estate, and DivcoWest. The stock traded 3.1% below the deal price at the time of publication.
  • 11:00 a.m. The New York Federal Reserve will release its 1-Year Inflation Expectations figure report, derived from a monthly survey of consumers.

 

Tuesday - March 10

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Oracle (ORCL), AeroVironment (AVAV), and Nio (NIO). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Kohl's (KSS) and Domo (DOMO) after they report.
  • All day - Walker & Dunlop (WD) will host an Investor Day event to provide an update on the company’s long-term growth strategy, with a specific focus on its five-year outlook.
  • All day - The three-day Barclays Global Healthcare Conference begins. Some of the notable companies with presentations or fireside chats include Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Baxter (BAX), and Medline (MDLN).
  • All day - The NEXT Summit New York conference focused on online gambling and sports betting will begin. BetMGM (MGM) (GMVHF) CEO Adam Greenblatt is one of the confirmed speakers.
  • All day - TransUnion (TRU) will host an Investor Day event.

 

Wednesday - March 11

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Campbell's Company (CPB), UiPath (PATH), and Netskope (NTSK). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Fossil (FOSL) and Bumble (BMBL) after they report.
  • All day - OPEC will release its monthly oil market report.
  • 8:30 a.m. The Consumer Price Index report for February will be released by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. Economists expect the core inflation rate to be 3.1%.
  • 10:00 a.m. Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) CEO Michel Doukeris will give a keynote speech at the UBS Global Consumer Retail Conference in New York City.
  • 10:00 a.m. Celsius Holdings (CELH) CFO Jarrod Langhans will present at the UBS Global Consumer Retail Conference in New York City.

 

Thursday - March 12

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Adobe (ADBE), Dollar General (DG), Ulta Beauty (ULTA), Lennar (LEN), Li Auto (LI), Rubrik (RBRK), and Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS). Options trading implies double-digit swings for Sleep Number (SNBR) and Once Upon A Farm (OFRM) after they report.
  • All day - Lucid Group (LCID) will host an Investor Day event that will include a comprehensive update on the company's strategic priorities, operational execution, long-term growth roadmap, and financial outlook.
  • As part of the program, the company will provide analysts and journalists with an early, in-depth preview of its upcoming midsize vehicle program and highlight select software advancements supporting its next-generation vehicle architecture.
  • All day - The six-day SXSW Conference in Austin, Texas begins. Major sponsors this year include Eli Lilly (LLY) and Samsung Electronics (SSNLF). Serve Robotics (SERV) will display new AI and robot technology.
  • All day - EPAM Systems (EPAM) will host an Investor Day event in Boston.
  • All day - Vulcan Materials Company (VMC) will hold its Investor Day event.
  • 8:00 a.m. Best Buy (BBY) CEO Corie Barry and CFO Matt Bilunas will present at the UBS Global Consumer Retail Conference in New York City.
  • 8:30 a.m. The February Producer Price Index report will be released.
  • 9:00 a.m. Mattel (MAT) CEO Ynon Kreiz will give a keynote speech at the UBS Global Consumer Retail Conference in New York City.

 

Friday - March 13

  • 8:30 a.m. The January Durable Goods Orders report will be released.
  • 8:30 a.m. The core PCE price index report for January will be released. Economists forecast a 3.1% increase.

data.title: U.S. consumer credit rises less than expected in January
data.description:
U.S. consumer credit rose by $8.05B in January, compared to the $11.1B gain expected and $25.20B (revised from $24.05B) rise logged in December, according to data released by the Federal Reserve on Friday.

Developing... Check back for updates.

data.title: Microsoft, Google, Amazon say Anthropic products still available despite Pentagon blacklist
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT), Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) and Amazon (AMZN) have confirmed that they will keep startup Anthropic’s (ANTHRO) artificial intelligence technology embedded in their products for clients, excluding the U.S. Department of War, CNBC reported. 

Google and Amazon told the news outlet on Friday, while Microsoft's comments were issued on Thursday.

Earlier on Thursday, the federal agency informed Anthropic that it would label the company a supply-chain risk. Anthropic subsequently said it intends to challenge the move in court.

Last week U.S. President Donald Trump called for federal agencies to drop their use of Anthropic, and Secretary of War Pete Hegseth said Anthropic would keep providing its services to the Pentagon for no more than six months. CNBC has confirmed that Anthropic models played a role in U.S. airstrikes on Iran in recent days.

Microsoft is the first major company to say it will keep working with Anthropic after the Pentagon’s actions. Some defense technology companies have also reportedly told employees to stop using Anthropic’s Claude models and migrate to alternatives.

“Our lawyers have studied the designation and have concluded that Anthropic products, including Claude, can remain available to our customers — other than the Department of War — through platforms such as M365, GitHub, and Microsoft’s AI Foundry and that we can continue to work with Anthropic on non-defense-related projects,” a Microsoft spokesperson told CNBC.

(This story has been updated to reflect Amazon's comments.)

data.title: Tylenol use in pregnancy fell after Trump’s autism warning: study
data.description:
Tylenol prescriptions for pregnant women dropped after President Donald Trump linked the Kenvue (KVUE)-made painkiller to autism in children, according to a study published in the British medical journal The Lancet.

The peer-reviewed analysis was based on emergency room visits by pregnant and non-pregnant women before and after Sept. 22, when Trump, in a White House briefing, told pregnant women to “fight like hell not to take" the over-the-counter medication.

During the study period after Sept. 22, the researchers noticed a 10% decline in prescriptions for acetaminophen, the active ingredient in Tylenol, among pregnant women aged 15–44 years.

The maximum decline of 20% occurred during the third week after an initial drop of 16%, while there was no such change in acetaminophen use among non-pregnant females during the period, the researchers added.

The findings were based on nearly 90K emergency department visits by pregnant women and approximately 853K emergency department visits by non-pregnant females.

"This means that thousands ​of women did not have their pain or fever treated in ERs, likely because they were needlessly afraid," said Jeremy Faust, an emergency physician at Brigham and Women’s Hospital in Boston and a co-author of the study.

"We think that's unfortunate because, among the options for pain control and ‌fever ⁠reduction, Tylenol is the safest option," Reuters reported, quoting Faust.

The same study ​analyzed outpatient prescription data for leucovorin, which Trump touted at the same briefing as a treatment for children with autistic symptoms. The B vitamin-based drug was once manufactured and marketed by GSK (GSK).

Using data from more than 8.6M outpatient visits by children aged 5–17 years, the researchers found that leucovorin prescriptions for the age group increased by 71% during the 11-week study period.

"In a time in which public trust in health, medicine, and science is under attack, it's regrettable that so many families will have been misled into thinking that this medication could somehow miraculously change the lives of children with autism," Faust said.

data.title: Dow upgraded at J.P. Morgan, seeing higher prices ahead for ethylene derivatives
data.description:
Dow Inc. (DOW) shares hovered between small gains and losses on Friday as J.P. Morgan upgraded the chemicals producer to Overweight from Neutral with a $40 price target, raised from $26, saying near-term polyethylene prices can rise sharply from higher energy prices caused by the restriction of oil and polyethylene shipments from the Middle East through the Strait of Hormuz.

Weak demand in China and Europe, global capacity additions in each of Dow’s (DOW) three business segments, higher interest rates, and a slow global economy have eroded the company's EBITDA and share price, but JPM's Jeffrey Zekauskas noted the stock offers a 4.2% dividend yield, a cyclically low earnings profile, and leverage to lower interest rates and higher oil prices, adding that each $10/bbl change in the Brent price probably affects Dow's (DOW) annual EBITDA by ~$1B. 

Oil prices have been rising, and prices for naphtha, the key feedstock for ethylene and its derivatives, also are rising sharply, jumping by $142/ton to $775/ton since last Thursday, the analyst said, which translates to a cost increase of ~$0.20/lb for the high cost producers, all things being equal.

A $0.20 increase in ethylene costs is a theoretical benefit to Dow (DOW) of ~$4B in EBITDA on an annual basis, Zekauskas calculated, adding that ethylene derivatives also likely would rise if oil and naphtha prices spiral higher.

Dow (DOW) trades at a discount relative to peers, the analyst said, with Dow valued at 9.7x JPM's FY 2026 EBITDA forecast and 7.7x to the FY 2027 forecast, compared to 11.7x and 10.2x, respectively, for Westlake (WLK) and 12.2x and 10.6x, respectively, for LyondellBasell (LYB).

data.title: Evaxion outlines expanded cash runway into 2H 2027 and anticipates data milestones for EVX-01 and EVX-04
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Evaxion A/S (EVAX) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Helen Tayton-Martin, newly appointed CEO, introduced the call and highlighted, “In 2025, we were very pleased to report tremendous progress across all pillars of the company.” Key business developments included the Merck (MSD) decision to exercise its option over the EVX-B3 program candidate, which Tayton-Martin described as “the first in-licensing, to our knowledge, of an infectious disease vaccine candidate identified and validated through an AI discovery platform.” Tayton-Martin noted retention of full rights to the EVX-B2 gonorrhea candidate after MSD declined its option, with “significant interest” still present for this program. She also emphasized a new collaboration with the Gates Foundation for a polio vaccine and broader interest in pipeline programs.
  • Tayton-Martin reported, “We were also honored by the recognition of our AI-Immunology platform by the Galien Foundation for AI advances in human health.” She confirmed a cash runway “to the second half of 2027,” bolstered by financing, business development, and use of their ATM facility.
  • Birgitte Rono, Chief Scientific Officer, explained, “2025 marked a turning point with significant advancement across our R&D pipeline and also -- and our AI platform.” Rono highlighted EVX-01's strong Phase II clinical data in melanoma and the new off-the-shelf AML vaccine candidate EVX-04, plus progress with the AI platform’s automated vaccine design module.
  • Thomas Schmidt, CFO, stated, “We have, throughout 2025, been really successful in expanding on our cash runway and also strengthening on our equity side… all summing up to a cash inflow of USD 32 million.” Schmidt added, “Net loss for the year, $7.7 million, certainly a bettering compared to last year.” He confirmed an end-year cash position of $23 million.

Outlook

  • Tayton-Martin outlined 2026 milestones including updates on EVX-01 with additional biomarker and immunogenicity data, and 3-year clinical data later in the year. She also expects data on EVX-B4 for Group A Streptococcus and submission of a regulatory application for EVX-04 in AML by year-end 2026. Tayton-Martin stated, “We will be talking more about the autoimmune applications of our AI-Immunology platform.”
  • The company plans to conduct future trials for key assets in partnership to maximize patient impact.

Financial Results

  • Schmidt detailed that the year’s financial highlights included a $32 million cash inflow from public offering, ATM use, MSD exercise fee, and warrant exercises. He reported, “Revenue has improved, but also we've improved on our operational costs.” Net loss for the year was $7.7 million, with a year-end cash position of $23 million and a cash runway into the second half of 2027. Schmidt added, “We have during 2025, established a far better foundation that really makes us -- puts us in a good position to continue our execution of strategy and business for '26 and the years beyond.”

Q&A

  • Thomas Flaten, Lake Street Capital Markets: Asked about strategic changes under new CEO. Tayton-Martin responded, “the fundamentals of Evaxion remained really strong… I think a sharpening and the deepening of focus around the assets that will have the most value.”
  • Flaten: Queried the need for clinical data to attract partners for EVX-04. Tayton-Martin replied, “We are certainly preparing to take it into the clinic… The stronger the value proposition to an external partner, and that's obviously what we're all about is maintaining the -- building the value for as long as we can.”
  • Michael Okunewitch, Maxim Group: Inquired about partnering efforts for EVX-01 and key data for partners. Tayton-Martin said the “updated clinical data package” and “translational data… continues to show why and how the immune response is happening.” Rono added, “the ability of our AI-Immunology platform to identify the relevant targets is getting a lot of interest from potential partners.”
  • Okunewitch: Asked about AI-Immunology applications in autoimmune disease. Rono shared, “We have done a lot of analysis on most prevalent autoimmune diseases, and we do see a clear fit for our platform… the majority is already in AI-Immunology.”
  • Swayampakula Ramakanth, H.C. Wainwright: Asked about translating technical capability to transactions. Tayton-Martin noted, “this is about crystallizing the offering through the validation of the candidates we have and sort of being in dialogue with the right people.”
  • Ramakanth: Asked about the Merck EVX-B2 option decision. Tayton-Martin said, “we remain really excited about the data… how and why Merck wanted to do that work? Or -- is something we can't really add any more commentary on.”
  • Ramakanth: Inquired about T-cell exhaustion markers in EVX-01. Rono replied, “we do a deep T-cell profiling, looking at activation markers, exhaustion markers and also at different phenotypes of the T-cells.”
  • Danya Ben-Hail, JonesTrading: Asked about validation strategy for autoimmune candidates. Rono explained, “we are now narrowing down which one could be the most… interesting… next step is to focus on building the additional smaller units… in parallel, we are also setting up mouse models in our lab.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a constructive but probing tone, frequently seeking clarification on partnership strategies and the validation path for novel assets, with questions on deal-making, clinical milestones, and data relevance. There was keen interest in the AI-Immunology platform’s differentiation.
  • Management’s tone was confident during prepared remarks, emphasizing progress and future milestones. In Q&A, responses were measured, occasionally deflecting specifics related to partnership negotiations, but reiterated confidence in the platform and pipeline. Tayton-Martin reflected, “I think the fundamental strengths and core of what Evaxion has to offer is even stronger now than potentially before.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management appeared more focused on partnership execution and specific clinical milestones, with management displaying increased confidence in financial positioning and asset potential.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter reflected continuity in strategy, but with greater emphasis on operational execution and financial stability. The appointment of a new CEO brought a sharpened focus on key value-driving assets, as opposed to major strategic shifts.
  • Guidance language shifted to highlight new 2026 milestones, such as regulatory submission for EVX-04 and expanded biomarker data for EVX-01, in contrast to the previous quarter’s focus on recent clinical data presentations and the MSD deal.
  • Management’s tone demonstrated increased confidence in both pipeline progress and the company’s financial standing, while analysts pressed for clarity on the execution path and partnership deal timing.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged the complexity of R&D collaborations and the unpredictability in advancing deals with large pharma partners. Tayton-Martin indicated, “sometimes is sort of R&D programs when they are back and forth and shared between organizations don't always run to plan.”
  • The company faces typical risks associated with clinical development, regulatory submissions, and the need to secure partnerships for later-stage development.
  • Analysts expressed concerns about the timing and certainty of partnerships and the path to monetization for the AI-Immunology platform.

Final Takeaway

Evaxion’s management highlighted a year of progress with key milestones in both pipeline advancement and financial stability, supported by platform validation through partnerships and clinical data. The company signaled a focus on deepening value in its most promising assets, preparing for major data and regulatory milestones in 2026, while maintaining a strong cash runway into the second half of 2027. Ongoing business development discussions and further validation of the AI-Immunology platform remain at the forefront as Evaxion targets expanded partnership opportunities and strategic growth in the coming year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: The oil price shock is a temporary disruption; markets will recover soon – Rosenberg Research
data.description:
David Rosenberg, founder of Rosenberg Research, says the recent spike in oil prices (CL1:COM), (CO1:COM) driven by the Iran conflict is a temporary disruption that will eventually fade.

While acknowledging the “rapidity of the move” in WTI prices, Rosenberg maintains that geopolitically induced oil shocks historically lack staying power.

“To everybody who’s watching, I say this too shall pass,” he stated.

In an interview with CNBC, Rosenberg drew comparisons to previous oil price spikes, including the 2022 surge following Russia’s invasion of Ukraine when WTI reached $120, and the 1990-1991 Iraq War when prices doubled. In both cases, he noted, markets eventually recovered, and “nobody talks about it today.”

Rosenberg explained that these price spikes contain the seeds of their own reversal through demand destruction.

“The one thing that we always know about these geopolitically induced oil price spasms is that they don’t typically have any staying power,” he said. “Because what ends up happening is it squeezes real incomes and purchasing power, and that demand destruction ends up sowing the seeds of the demise for that oil price increase.”

For the near term, Rosenberg acknowledged the spike creates “short term pain at the pump” for consumers, describing current market conditions as “nail-biting time” for traders.

He noted that upcoming income tax refunds will help keep the consumer sector “intact,” though those funds will likely go toward necessities rather than vacations or luxury goods.

The modern economy is better positioned to absorb oil shocks than it was decades ago, Rosenberg noted, pointing to the shift toward a more service-driven economy and lower energy intensity.

However, he cautioned against dismissing the impact entirely, particularly for truckers and consumers facing higher retail gas prices.

Looking ahead, Rosenberg suggested that if the conflict lasts weeks rather than months, markets will move past this disruption within three to four months. He emphasized that oil prices historically follow a “sine wave” pattern, with spikes eventually returning to more normal levels.

Oil ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (DBO), (OILK), and (USL).

Natural Gas ETFs: (UNG), (BOIL), and (UNL).
